text.title: Bug Report Quality Rubric Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example: an LLM-output bug runs through the rubric scorer, fails on AI context, gets blocked, the reporter fixes the missing fields, and a follow-up scoring run accepts it.

section.title: Input — initial report (would-be-blocked)
section.code:
Title: AI summary is wrong
The AI gave a bad summary of my doc. It said "the document is about cats" but it was about dogs.
Severity: high Priority: high
Browser: latest Chrome

section.title: Scorer output — block
section.code: { "artefact_id": "brq-issue-4429", "ticket_ref": "issue-4429", "verdict": "block-need-ai-context", "score": 28, "is_ai_feature": true, "dimensions": {"title":3,"repro":2,"expected_vs_actual":8,"environment":2,"severity":7,"priority":7,"evidence":0,"ai_context":0}, "field_fixes": [ {"field":"title","issue":"vague","suggested_fix":"<surface>: <verb> + <observable>, e.g. 'Doc summariser: emits cat-summary for dog-content input'"}, {"field":"environment","issue":"'latest' is not pinnable","suggested_fix":"Paste output of: chrome://version, and the app build SHA from /version endpoint"}, {"field":"ai_context","issue":"prompt+model+seed missing","suggested_fix":"Click 'Export conversation' in the AI panel and attach the JSON"}, {"field":"evidence","issue":"no screenshot or log","suggested_fix":"Attach the AI panel screenshot showing the wrong summary"} ], "version": "1.0.0", "last_reviewed": "2026-05-22" }
